---
title: "Districtwise Education Sampling Analysis"
format: html
---

#
```{python}
import pandas as pd
data=pd.read_csv("C:/Users/Udith P Kidiyoor/Downloads/education_districtwise.csv")
data.head(10)
```
#
```{python}
data.info()
```
#
```{python}
data.shape
```
#
```{python}
data.describe()
```
#
```{python}
data.isna().sum()
```
#
```{python}
data=data.dropna()
```
#
```{python}
sampled_data=data.sample(n=50, replace=True, random_state=31208)
sampled_data
```
### Insights-
The output shows 50 districts selected randomly from your dataset. Each has a different literacy rate, but note that row 254 was sampled twice, which is possible because you sampled with replacement.
#
```{python}
estimate1=sampled_data["OVERALL_LI"].mean()
estimate1
```
#
```{python}
estimate2=data["OVERALL_LI"].sample(n=50, replace=True, random_state=56810).mean()
estimate2
```
### Insights
For your second estimate, the sample mean for district literacy rate is about 74.25%.

Due to sampling variability, this sample mean is different from the sample mean of your previous estimate, 74.22% – but they’re really close.
#
```{python}
estimate_list=[]
for i in range(10000):
    estimate_list.append(data["OVERALL_LI"].sample(n=50, replace=True).mean())
estimate_df=pd.DataFrame({"estimate":estimate_list})
```
```{python}
estimate_df.describe()
```
#
```{python}
sample_mean=estimate_df.mean()
sample_mean
```
#
```{python}
population_mean=data["OVERALL_LI"].mean()
population_mean
```
#
```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))
sns.histplot(
    estimate_df["estimate"],
    color="darkorange",
    kde=True
)
plt.axvline(
    x=estimate_df["estimate"].mean(),
    color="green",
    label="Mean of Sample Means"
)
plt.axvline(
    x=population_mean,
    color="red",
    label="Population Mean"
)
plt.title("Sampling Distribution")
plt.xlabel("Sample Mean")
plt.ylabel("Density")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
